rows.locks: Re-verify requires openchrome, scenario verdicts, the four gates plus design-integrity, rollback, kill-switch, source-apply, and trust-gradient evidence before routing.
Low-risk reversible proposals auto-apply only after re-verify passes, trust-gradient returns auto-applied, no human input is required, and after evidence improves the attributed milestone.
A regressing post-apply re-verify is rejected and records the existing rollback handle rather than promoting the fix.
Verified reversible high-risk/source-affecting proposals queue for one-click human go/no-go provenance and never auto-apply source mutation.
Failed gates, hidden human input, new data plane drift, and irreversible proposals fail closed before any autonomous auto-apply route.
The demo chain covers detect, explain, trace, attribute, propose, re-verify, low-risk auto-apply, and high-risk queueing on the existing deckbuilder title.

Coverage v63 is test-only and preserves openchrome, scenario verdicts, the four gates plus design-integrity, journal.md, ledger.jsonl, loop-coverage attribution, evolve, source-apply, and trust-gradient boundaries.

boundary: Scenario Coverage v63 is test-only Rust regression coverage for the Era L M71 self-improvement loop over the existing openchrome/evidence pipeline: scenario verdicts, the four gates plus design-integrity, journal.md, ledger.jsonl, loop-coverage attribution, evolve, source-apply, trust-gradient, rollback, and kill-switch. It introduces no new verification engine, no new data plane, and no new persistent store. The autonomous low-risk path completes with zero human input; unverified, irreversible, regressing, high-risk, or source-affecting fixes are never auto-applied. High-risk/source-affecting fixes remain queued for thin one-click human go/no-go provenance while unrelated autonomous work continues. Fun/taste and release go/no-go remain human Ring 2. #1 and #23 remain open.
